title: KVM ioctl probe — close the O_RDWR gap
Force -accel kvm — close the concurrent-VM race
note: THIRD TCG refinement. P0313 O_RDWR (4/7) → P0315 CREATE_VM ioctl (7/7 single-shot) → this: per-VM hard-fail. protocol-warm 3×QEMU concurrent: kvmCheck single-shot probe succeeds, 2/3 children race-lose CREATE_VM (host-level resource/LSM race). virtualisation.qemu.options=[-accel kvm] → no TCG fallback, QEMU hard-fails instead of silently degrading. Complements kvmCheck (earlier-fail), doesn't replace it. nixbuild.net frontend flag STALE (kvmEnabled=True + slurm kvm:y on all derivations; actual /dev/kvm state varies) — feedback mechanism would need external API coordination, out of scope. soft_dep P0304 (T10 _TCG_MARKERS). common.nix NOT top-30; P0314 non-overlapping.

title: ChannelSession::Drop abort() races EOF-cancel — graceful-shutdown signal
note: CORRECTNESS — P0331-T1-Q3 answer routed here: channel_close at server.rs:554 EXISTS (Design-B path) but ChannelSession::Drop :227-236 does proto_task.abort() — BROKEN. Race: channel_eof drops client_tx mpsc-sender → session.rs:107 EOF-arm → cancel loop; BUT channel_close fires close-behind → sessions.remove → Drop → abort. If abort wins (one scheduler-yield window), CancelBuild never fires. Fix T1+T2: CancellationToken in ChannelSession, Drop fires token.cancel() not abort(), session.rs selects on it, both paths converge on extracted cancel_active_builds(ctx, reason). T3 regression: token.cancel() → mock-scheduler received CancelBuild (mutation-check: revert to abort() → test fails). Adds r[gw.conn.cancel-on-disconnect] marker (owns it unconditionally — P0331 should NOT add). Narrow window but real. Orthogonal to P0331 build.rs:462 fix (that = is-map-empty; this = do-we-reach-iteration).

P0212 breadcrumb at gc_schedule.rs:229. T1 NEW rio-test-support/src/metrics.rs superset. T2-T4 migrate 8 files to imports. T5 Entry-crit note in P0328. SEQUENCE BEFORE P0328 (touches all 5 metrics_registered.rs, believes 4 are NEW). Net LOC negative (~-220L). No hard deps — pure extraction.

title: EOF-cancel ordering — does build.rs:462 premature remove leak builds?
note: CORRECTNESS INVESTIGATION (P0329 pattern). session.rs:107 EOF-cancel iterates active_build_ids — but handler/build.rs:462 removes build_id BEFORE error bubbles. Client disconnect mid-opcode → write fails → :462 removes → session.rs:107 iterates empty map → no CancelBuild → worker slot leak until backstop. BUT may be BY-DESIGN: :474-477 wraps Err→Ok(BuildResult::failure), :462 may be intentional for clean-error-path. T1 trace Q1/Q2/Q3 (BuildResult write path; what error type reaches session.rs; russh channel_close callback?). T2 routes: Design-A fix (conditional remove) OR Design-B doc (scope comment + spec marker). T3 regression test either way. gateway.md:717 says CancelBuild on disconnect is INTENT — question is whether both code paths serve it. Dep P0240 is scheduling.nix cancel-timing VM — natural home for e2e T3 but unit-ish mock-scheduler version works independently.

title: Migration 017 — tenant_keys FK missing ON DELETE → CASCADE
note: Bughunter: 014:14 REFERENCES tenants(tenant_id) has NO ON DELETE — PG default NO ACTION. Only silent-default tenant-FK (009 SET NULL, 012 CASCADE, 015 RESTRICT all explicit). DELETE FROM tenants fails on any tenant_keys row incl REVOKED. Latent (no tenant-delete code exists yet) but P0256 will hit. CASCADE matches 012 path_tenants pattern (tenant_keys OWNED BY tenant; SET NULL violates NOT NULL). 014 shipped+checksummed → new 017 DROP+ADD CONSTRAINT. T2 test has precondition self-check (pre-count==2 before delete). Mutation check: run T2 against 016-only DB → DELETE fails with FK error. Sequence before P0256.
